task: Write a Python function third_largest_distinct(nums) returning the third largest distinct value in the list, or None when there are fewer than three distinct values. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function correctly implements the requested logic, with some minor room for improvement in conciseness.
trace: I first ensure that the input list only contains unique values by using a set. Then I sort the list of unique values in descending order, which automatically puts them in their respective ranks (smallest to largest). If there are fewer than three distinct values, I return None to indicate this edge case. Otherwise, I return the third largest value from the sorted list.
